channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - kotaku.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for kotaku.com

item.title: Reckless Ben Shares ‘Final Message’ Regarding Episode 3 Of Bricks & Minifigs Lego Drama: ‘I Can’t Post It, Or Else I’ll Go To Jail’
item.content:encoded:
Reckless Ben’s investigation into the Mansell family’s missing $200,000 Lego Star Wars collection has hit a seemingly immovable force, as he’s announced that he’s now no longer able to release the third and final part of his series regarding Lego resellers Bricks & Minifigs after being served a TRO (temporary restraining order) by the Utah Fourth Judicial District Court.

The last time we left Reckless Ben’s Lego investigation, things were seemingly looking up for both Ben and the Mansell family. The CEO of Patreon stepped in to personally ensure that Reckless Ben’s content would remain on the platform, unredacted bodycam footage of American Fork Police Department’s involvement had allegedly leaked online, and the GoFundMe set up to reimburse the Mansell family for their missing Lego Star Wars collection had reached over $450,000.

Given the way things seemed to be going, Ben’s fans are understandably not too happy about the latest development in his crusade against Bricks & Minifigs Franchising (BAMF). As Ben explained in the latest video on his channel, published earlier today on June 9, a judge has allowed BAMF to serve him with legal papers via his email address, which means he can no longer publish (or even talk about) the final part of his investigation.

As Ben explained in his latest video, releasing Part 3 of his investigation wouldn’t just get him in legal trouble, it would also result in the loss of the Mansell family’s GoFundMe: “Normally I would be like, ‘screw the big guy telling me what to do, I’m gonna do what’s morally right.’ But, in this situation, if I do that, then all my friends get screwed with this lawsuit, and we lose all this GoFundMe money we raised, like, immediately.”

He’s not bluffing either, as the TRO, dated May 28, 2026, specifically states that Ben can no longer release any videos relating to Bricks & Minifigs Franchising. In fact, it specifically states that even Ben’s previous videos may be removed as well, due to the fact that they contain information that relates “to the private legal dispute underlying this matter between Bryan [Mansell] and Chrystal [Law, one of the previous owners of the Bricks & Minifigs location in Salem, Oregon] and the assertions of wrongdoing as described in this Verified Complaint.”

Still, I have a feeling this isn’t the end for Reckless Ben’s investigation. After all, he doesn’t seem like the type to let something like a court-issued TRO stop him. Considering the CEO of Patreon was so keen to help last week, maybe he could post it on Ben’s behalf?



https://kotaku.com/reckless-ben-lego-star-wars-part-3-investigation-bricks-minifigs-2000704363
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Zelda: Ocarina Of Time Teaser Has The Internet Joking That Nintendo Is Entering Its ‘Hire This Man’ Era
item.content:encoded:
Today, during Nintendo’s big Summer Game Fest-adjacent Direct, the company officially revealed its much-rumored upcoming Legend of Zelda: Ocarina of Time remake. And a large portion of the internet reacted by joking that it looked a lot like those fan-created, often not-great-looking Unreal Engine remake videos of Zelda games, the ones that routinely have people saying in the comments that Nintendo should “hire this man.”

On June 9, Nintendo revealed a very short teaser for its upcoming remake of the Nintendo 64 classic The Legend of Zelda: Ocarina of Time. We didn’t get to see much beyond a few seconds of what Link will look like in the remake, set to launch on Switch 2 sometime in 2026. But those few seconds were enough for people to start comparing this new, more realistic-looking version of the famous hero with how he’s often seen in fan-created remakes and trailers built using Unreal Engine or similar tools.

And sure, we’ve only seen a few seconds, and it could look much better when we see more of the game later this year, but that didn’t stop people from having some fun online or complaining about the art direction of the remake.

Over the years, these fan-made videos have become a meme online as they often lack the charm and character of the original Zelda games they are remaking, and still have people commenting below that Nintendo should “hire this man” or learn something from the video’s creator. With the new Link looking a lot like those versions, a lot of people are joking that Nintendo listened and did indeed hire that man after all. Some even pointed to Nintendo’s upcoming Star Fox remake as also featuring “hire this man graphics.” Others referenced the “Focus Up, M” meme, too.

2016: Nintendo Hire This Man
2026: Nintendo Hired This Man https://t.co/Uf7DyLp4rx pic.twitter.com/hoKNakKMhy

— Pinguis (@Pinguis54) June 9, 2026

why is ocarina of time in hire this man graphics

— William (@DooMYouTube) June 9, 2026

NINTENDO HIRE THIS MAN pic.twitter.com/js5sSkcZbI

— Johann (@LookAtMyMeat1) June 9, 2026

I’m sorry but I really can’t get over that it looks like Nintendo finally hired this man pic.twitter.com/Wr8qnRhcog

— Arty the Spectacular Bun 🐇 (@SuperiorGothBun) June 9, 2026

"Nintendo hire this man" ass trailer

— Alejandro (@Alejandro101524) June 9, 2026

You can’t even post the Focus, M video anymore they’re just making that the look

— august (@regularaugust) June 9, 2026

If you look at this and the new Star Fox Nintendo are seriously starting to hire That Man https://t.co/uhDn8Vr57V

— H20: 2nd Impact (@I__HATE_SOCCER) June 9, 2026

NINTENDO HIRE THIS MAN pic.twitter.com/0offXSlAOF

— Snugboy (@RealSnugboy) June 9, 2026

“Nintendo hire this man!” pic.twitter.com/PiBa6KYlLP

— G*rre (@springbris) June 9, 2026



https://kotaku.com/zelda-ocarina-of-time-teaser-has-the-internet-joking-that-nintendo-is-entering-its-hire-this-man-era-2000704454
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot
